codeReview.summary: Reviewed the feature definition, handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, and the current `tests/test_data_integrity.py`, `tests/test_antileak.py`, `tests/test_feature_engineering.py`, plus the shared fixture/helper code they rely on. The feature does add the three requested pytest modules and stays fixture-backed instead of calling live APIs, but the core data-integrity and anti-leakage assertions are aimed at a synthetic parquet generator rather than the real training-data builder/cached dataset, so the suite can pass while the production training-data path regresses.

issues.description: `cached_training_data()` builds its own parquet through `_write_cached_training_validation_fixture()` (`tests/test_data_builder.py:310-347`) and every integrity assertion then runs against that manufactured frame. This means the new tests prove only that the helper emits unique `game_pk` values, regular-season rows, and non-null targets; they do not validate the actual `build_training_dataset()` / cached training-data path that the feature contract was meant to protect. A regression in the real builder that reintroduced duplicate game IDs, spring/postseason rows, or NaN targets would still leave this file green.

The 100-game anti-leakage coverage samples the same synthetic fixture and then calls `assert_training_data_is_leakage_free()`, which only checks `as_of_timestamp < scheduled_start` (`src/model/data_builder.py:490-503`). Because the test never builds or loads real training rows, it cannot catch leakage in the actual feature values (for example same-day doubleheader contamination) even though the mission’s anti-leakage requirement is about production training data, not handcrafted timestamps. In practice this test can pass even if the real builder leaks same-day information while still stamping rows with a prior-day `as_of_timestamp`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `pipeline-worker` skill is still pipeline-runtime oriented and does not map cleanly to automation-testing features that are test-only, so workers reasonably deviate from the documented procedure on features like t28.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/pipeline-worker/SKILL.md:23-45` prescribes pipeline/scheduler implementation and manual `src.pipeline.daily` verification, while the handoff `C:\Users\bills\.factory\missions\83c0c194-72d1-4821-8b08-68a3497c3590\handoffs\2026-03-20T07-32-24-084Z__t28-data-integrity-tests__963532a9-e879-4491-bdb4-049f61d71168.json:97-106` records `followedProcedure: false` and explicitly suggests a test-focused variant of the skill for pytest-only automation-testing work.

summary: Fail. The new files exist and avoid live APIs, but the two core validation areas—training-data integrity and 100-game anti-leakage—only exercise a synthetic fixture generator, not the real training-data builder/cached dataset, so the feature does not fully deliver the requested regression protection.
